In this lesson, I'm going to cover how to collaborate with macro influences to maximise results. This is important because often working with a macro influencer will be a sizable investment, so we want to make sure that we have all of the tools on hand to get the highest ROI possible. It's important to understand that the way that you collaborate with macro influences will differ somewhat from the way that you engage micro influences. If you use the same collaboration techniques across the board, you're not going to be able to leverage the true power of macro influences. Starting with attributing ROI from your macro influencer collaborations. The problem with Instagram, which we all know is that in an organic post, you can't put a clickable link. So you're going to see the most of that traffic channeling back in through your direct or organic search. That's where you're going to get the most uplift. A far better way to attribute sales is using personalized discount codes with your influencer collaborations, which I'm sure a lot of you are already familiar with, but I'm going to run through it quickly to rehash as this is an important one. This is the technique in which you offer paid macro influences, a personalized discount code for their followers to use on your e-commerce store. For example, my code could be greater 25 for my followers to save 25% off from my store or $25 off a single skew. This is obviously great for tracking as you can directly attribute sales to that influencer. You might have seen brands like Daniel Wellington, Movement Watchers, High Smile and Elias Kinn doing this on Instagram and YouTube. And this doesn't only just have to be a discount code. It can be pretty much anything that incentivizes an influencer's followers to follow through and make a purchase on your store. It could include things like free shipping, store vouchers, a flash sale or a free gift with purchase, which will help your goal of driving sales to your store. And these can be offered within the influencer's personalized code rather than a discount, which is often forgotten about. The goal is to get influencers to encourage their followers to buy then and there on the spot without being too pushy. So get your influencers to use some scarcity and FOMO where possible. Like last time I posted this product, it sold out an X amount of time, so I'd get in quick if I were you. Or that you don't have much stock left, so get in quick so you don't miss out. Outside of incentivizing a purchase, you could also look at affiliate marketing and longer term partnerships with influencers, which I'll cover more in a couple of lessons time. I call this a cluster because this is a group of influencers that are drawn together or interconnected for a reason. ## [06:01 - 09:02]

recreate their top performing content for your brand. Most social platforms will have analytics around top performing content. On Instagram, for example, you simply ask them to go into their insights then under the content tab hit see all under feed posts. This will take them to their top performing posts over the last year ranked by impressions and engagements. Get the influencer to screenshot this page and you may be able to identify trends in this content. You may notice things like three out of the top nine performing posts within a bathtub or featured strawberries or puppies. So finally, let's finish this lesson on a high and talk about one of my favorite giveaway techniques, the tag to win giveaway. And if you've done start and scale, you'll have already heard about this one. But I have some more examples for you in a recent case study with calming blankets, which is a brand that I advise. So just as a little refresher in case this concept is new to you, a tag to win giveaway is a giveaway in which the influencer asks their followers to tag a friend on their post and following a brand to go in the draw to win a prize. For example, the influencer's caption could be something like, Hey guys, I've turned out with skinny me tea to bring you this incredible health prize pack value to over $1,000 for you and your best friend. To enter all you need to do is tag a friend on this post and both follow at Greta and at SMT official to be in the draw to win. One comment equals one entry and you can tag one friend per comment. The winner will be announced in 24 hours. Some pro tips here are to include a strong clear call to action and limit the time span of the campaign to create a sense of urgency and inspire action. Don't ask their followers to do too much. The easier it is to enter then and there on the spot, the better. This strategy is going to work better with macro influencers for larger prizes and micro influencers for smaller more attainable prizes like winner 28 day tea talks for you and your friend. Remember each time somebody tags a friend on the post, it's an opportunity for another person to be introduced to your brand by a friend. In terms of tracking here, you want to focus on engagements, especially comments and social follower growth. If you have a business account on Facebook, YouTube or Instagram for example, you'll be able to see audience growth. But it's also a good idea to note down the number of followers you have before the post went live and after the campaign has finished to compare results. These giveaways aren't only a great way to increase your account's social following,
